item.title: Online Self-Calibration Against Hallucination in Vision-Language Models
item.description: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) often suffer from hallucinations, generating descriptions that include visual details absent from the input image. Recent preference alignment methods typically rely on supervision distilled from stro...

item.title: Let ViT Speak: Generative Language-Image Pre-training
item.description: In this paper, we present Generative Language-Image Pre-training (GenLIP), a minimalist generative pretraining framework for Vision Transformers (ViTs) designed for multimodal large language models (MLLMs). To better align vision encoder...

item.title: UniVidX: A Unified Multimodal Framework for Versatile Video Generation via Diffusion Priors
item.description: Recent progress has shown that video diffusion models (VDMs) can be repurposed for diverse multimodal graphics tasks. However, existing methods often train separate models for each problem setting, which fixes the input-output mapping an...

item.title: Stable-GFlowNet: Toward Diverse and Robust LLM Red-Teaming via Contrastive Trajectory Balance
item.description: Large Language Model (LLM) Red-Teaming, which proactively identifies vulnerabilities of LLMs, is an essential process for ensuring safety. Finding effective and diverse attacks in red-teaming is important, but achieving both is challengi...

item.title: Themis: Training Robust Multilingual Code Reward Models for Flexible Multi-Criteria Scoring
item.description: Reward models (RMs) have become an indispensable fixture of the language model (LM) post-training playbook, enabling policy alignment and test-time scaling. Research on the application of RMs in code generation, however, has been compara...

item.title: MASCing: Configurable Mixture-of-Experts Behavior via Activation Steering Masks
item.description: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures in Large Language Models (LLMs) have significantly reduced inference costs through sparse activation. However, this sparse activation paradigm also introduces new safety challenges. Since only a sub...

item.title: From Skill Text to Skill Structure: The Scheduling-Structural-Logical Representation for Agent Skills
item.description: LLM agents increasingly rely on reusable skills, capability packages that combine instructions, control flow, constraints, and tool calls. In most current agent systems, however, skills are still represented by text-heavy artifacts, incl...

item.title: Trees to Flows and Back: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models
item.description: Decision trees and diffusion models are ostensibly disparate model classes, one discrete and hierarchical, the other continuous and dynamic. This work unifies the two by establishing a crisp mathematical correspondence between hierarchic...

item.title: Learning to Act and Cooperate for Distributed Black-Box Consensus Optimization
item.description: Distributed blackbox consensus optimization is a fundamental problem in multi-agent systems, where agents must improve a global objective using only local objective queries and limited neighbor communication. Existing methods largely rel...

item.title: Talker-T2AV: Joint Talking Audio-Video Generation with Autoregressive Diffusion Modeling
item.description: Joint audio-video generation models have shown that unified generation yields stronger cross-modal coherence than cascaded approaches. However, existing models couple modalities throughout denoising via pervasive attention, treating high...

item.title: Web2BigTable: A Bi-Level Multi-Agent LLM System for Internet-Scale Information Search and Extraction
item.description: Agentic web search increasingly faces two distinct demands: deep reasoning over a single target, and structured aggregation across many entities and heterogeneous sources. Current systems struggle on both fronts. Breadth-oriented tasks d...

item.title: Prox-E: Fine-Grained 3D Shape Editing via Primitive-Based Abstractions
item.description: Text-based 2D image editing models have recently reached an impressive level of maturity, motivating a growing body of work that heavily depends on these models to drive 3D edits. While effective for appearance-based modifications, such...

item.title: Odysseus: Scaling VLMs to 100+ Turn Decision-Making in Games via Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Given the rapidly growing capabilities of vision-language models (VLMs), extending them to interactive decision-making tasks such as video games has emerged as a promising frontier. However, existing approaches either rely on large-scale...

item.title: When Do Diffusion Models learn to Generate Multiple Objects?
item.description: Text-to-image diffusion models achieve impressive visual fidelity, yet they remain unreliable in multi-object generation. Despite extensive empirical evidence of these failures, the underlying causes remain unclear. We begin by asking ho...

item.title: End-to-End Autoregressive Image Generation with 1D Semantic Tokenizer
item.description: Autoregressive image modeling relies on visual tokenizers to compress images into compact latent representations. We design an end-to-end training pipeline that jointly optimizes reconstruction and generation, enabling direct supervision...

item.title: AnalogRetriever: Learning Cross-Modal Representations for Analog Circuit Retrieval
item.description: Analog circuit design relies heavily on reusing existing intellectual property (IP), yet searching across heterogeneous representations such as SPICE netlists, schematics, and functional descriptions remains challenging. Existing methods...

item.title: Soft Anisotropic Diagrams for Differentiable Image Representation
item.description: We introduce Soft Anisotropic Diagrams (SAD), an explicit and differentiable image representation parameterized by a set of adaptive sites in the image plane. In SAD, each site specifies an anisotropic metric and an additively weighted d...

item.title: Better Models, Faster Training: Sigmoid Attention for single-cell Foundation Models
item.description: Training stable biological foundation models requires rethinking attention mechanisms: we find that using sigmoid attention as a drop in replacement for softmax attention a) produces better learned representations: on six diverse single-...

item.title: Map2World: Segment Map Conditioned Text to 3D World Generation
item.description: 3D world generation is essential for applications such as immersive content creation or autonomous driving simulation. Recent advances in 3D world generation have shown promising results; however, these methods are constrained by grid la...

item.title: Learning while Deploying: Fleet-Scale Reinforcement Learning for Generalist Robot Policies
item.description: Generalist robot policies increasingly benefit from large-scale pretraining, but offline data alone is insufficient for robust real-world deployment. Deployed robots encounter distribution shifts, long-tail failures, task variations, and...

item.title: LASE: Language-Adversarial Speaker Encoding for Indic Cross-Script Identity Preservation
item.description: A speaker encoder used in multilingual voice cloning should treat the same speaker identically regardless of which script the audio was uttered in. Off-the-shelf encoders do not, and the failure is accent-conditional. On a 1043-pair West...
